license.comment: MIT only; declared in the crate manifest and LICENSE.md.

documentation.comment: The older book at book.freyaui.dev is now marked 'no longer maintained'; docs.rs and the examples folder are the current reference. freyaui.dev is the landing site.

first-release.comment: First crates.io publish was 0.1.1 on November 19, 2022.

latest-stable-version.comment: 0.3.4 released 2025-06-02 is the last non-prerelease publish. 0.4.0 is in release-candidate phase (0.4.0-rc.19 on 2026-04-23) and ships a major rewrite that drops the Dioxus dependency.

maintenance-status.comment: 47 crate versions across 3+ years; 0.4 rewrite in active RC cycle with the most recent publish on 2026-04-23.

maturity.comment: No 1.0 release yet; 0.4 is a substantial architectural rewrite (dropping Dioxus for an in-house reactive core) and is still in RC. API remains in flux between minor versions.

corporate-backing.comment: Solo primary maintainer (Marc Espín / marc2332) funded through GitHub Sponsors; no company sits behind the project.

implementation.comment: Widget/layout/reactivity layers are pure Rust. The renderer links the Skia C++ library via the freya-skia-safe bindings, which is consistent with how other pure-Rust Rust GUI frameworks (Slint, Floem) are classified here.

rendering-mode.comment: Declarative components produce a persistent element tree that updates reactively when state changes; no immediate-mode redraw-from-scratch per frame.

reactivity-model.comment: Uses hooks (use_state, use_signal, use_memo) and rsx-style authoring. 0.4 moved from a Dioxus-powered reactive core to an in-house core (freya-core + ragnarok event system) but the hook/RSX authoring model is preserved.

binding-target.comment: Not a binding to a native widget toolkit; widgets are implemented in Rust and drawn to Skia.

thread-model.comment: Event loop and component tree live on the main thread, per the winit-based architecture; async work is offloaded via Tokio.

renderer.comment: Skia is the drawing API; it runs on top of an OpenGL (glutin) or Vulkan (ash) context depending on platform and features.

gpu-acceleration.comment: Skia draws via GPU (OpenGL/Vulkan) by default.

hidpi-support.comment: winit reports the display scale factor; Skia scales pixel-accurate rendering accordingly.

vector-rendering.comment: freya-skia-safe is configured with the `svg` feature; Skia supports vector primitives and SVG natively.

custom-shaders.comment: No documented public API for injecting custom GPU shader passes. Skia exposes SkRuntimeEffect internally, but Freya does not surface a shader-pass extension point in its public API.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: The examples folder contains feature_canvas.rs demonstrating custom per-frame drawing into a canvas element.

windows.comment: Windows is a first-class build target (both MSVC and GNU toolchains mentioned in contributing docs).

macos.comment: macOS (Apple Silicon and Intel) is a first-class build target.

linux-x11.comment: Linux is officially supported; winit handles X11 out of the box.

linux-wayland.comment: winit 0.30 supports Wayland natively; Freya does not disable it. No dedicated Wayland-tested status statement from the maintainers, but it is a standard winit target.

ios.comment: No official iOS target; the README explicitly advertises desktop-only support (Windows/macOS/Linux).

android.comment: A community contributor (SparkyTD) has done work toward Android, but the project is not advertised as supporting Android officially and it is not listed as a tested platform.

web-wasm.comment: Project tagline is 'cross-platform and non-web GUI library'; WebAssembly is explicitly out of scope.

embedded-no-std.comment: Depends on std-heavy crates (tokio, winit, skia). No no_std story.

authoring-style.comment: Primary authoring is via an rsx!-style macro inside Rust components. Examples in the repo use this macro uniformly. A chained builder API is shown on freyaui.dev but the documented/primary style is the macro.

hot-reload.comment: Earlier (Dioxus-based) versions inherited Dioxus hot-reload for static attributes. The 0.4 rewrite dropped the Dioxus dependency and the project does not currently advertise a hot-reload feature. Unverified for the current 0.4 RC series.

layout-system.comment: Uses an in-house layout engine called Torin with flexbox-style direction/main-align/cross-align primitives.

styling-approach.comment: Styles are authored as attributes on rsx elements (inline-props). A theming system provides reusable theme structs and light/dark switching.

theme-switching.comment: Runtime-swappable themes with light/dark variants ship in the standard library.

accessibility.comment: Depends directly on accesskit 0.24 and accesskit_winit for cross-platform screen-reader integration.

internationalization.comment: Optional `i18n` feature pulls in a Fluent-based localization layer; routing and text layout handle locale-aware content.

widget-breadth.comment: Broad built-in coverage: buttons, inputs, switches, sliders, scroll views, calendar, color picker, tooltip, table, markdown, rich text editor, code editor with tree-sitter, terminal emulator, Lucide icons, Material Design components, Plotters charts. Missing a proper data-grid virtualization story and richer dialog primitives keeps it at 4 rather than 5.

data-grid-table.comment: component_table example ships in the examples folder and a VirtualScrollView is available for large datasets.

rich-text-markdown.comment: Built-in markdown component plus a rich-text editor component with cursor/selection handling.

tree-outline.comment: No dedicated tree widget found in the built-in component set; apps typically compose nested VirtualScrollViews. Unverified.

native-menus.comment: component_menu.rs demonstrates an in-app menu drawn with Freya widgets; no OS-native menu bar / context menu integration is provided.

file-dialogs.comment: No first-party file dialog; apps reach for the standalone rfd crate.

charts-plotting.comment: Optional `plotters` feature provides first-class integration with the Plotters crate.

custom-widgets.comment: New components are ordinary Rust functions returning an rsx tree; hooks compose cleanly. Lower-level custom rendering requires going through Torin/Skia, which costs a star.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: Tokio is a direct dependency and the only documented async runtime integration; background tasks and remote asset loading go through Tokio.

system-tray.comment: No built-in tray icon support; not listed in features or examples.

notifications.comment: No native OS notification integration advertised.

clipboard.comment: Rich text and input components support clipboard cut/copy/paste via the winit-provided clipboard.

drag-and-drop.comment: component_drag_drop example demonstrates drag-and-drop within the UI.

embedded-webview.comment: Optional `webview` feature embeds a webview component alongside Freya widgets.

multi-window.comment: feature_multi_window example ships in the examples folder.

binary-size.comment: No reliable measurement available; a hello-world binary was not built for this research pass. Skia-based frameworks typically land in the moderate-to-large range, but this is not verified.

startup-time.comment: No first-party or reproducible third-party cold-start measurement found.

memory-footprint.comment: No first-party or reproducible third-party idle-RSS measurement found.

idle-redraw.comment: Reactive core redraws only when observed state changes; animation hooks opt into continuous redraw while running.

dependency-count.comment: Freya pulls in winit, glutin, ash, tokio, Skia bindings, and accesskit among others; total transitive count was not measured in this pass.

documentation-quality.comment: docs.rs API coverage is strong (~94%) and the examples folder is extensive. The dedicated book (book.freyaui.dev) is marked 'no longer maintained', which leaves a gap between API docs and a narrative guide for newcomers.

api-ergonomics.comment: rsx! macro, hooks, and signal/memoization APIs read idiomatically and avoid boilerplate. A point is held back because the 0.4 rewrite still has rough edges and breaking changes across RCs.

examples-count.comment: Well over 20 example files (100+ reported) covering components, animations, features, and platform integrations.

interactive-playground.comment: No online playground or demo gallery found; the landing site is marketing-only and the book is offline.

ide-tooling.comment: No Freya-specific LSP, formatter, or syntax package. Because views are written in plain Rust via rsx!, rust-analyzer provides baseline IDE support, but there is no dedicated tooling.

dev-tools-inspector.comment: Optional `devtools` feature / freya-devtools crate exposes a runtime component-tree inspector.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the top-level `freya` crate as reported by crates.io on 2026-04-24.

notable-production-users.comment: No shipping production applications publicly attributed to Freya. GitHub sponsors are listed but not product users.

community-size.comment: Active Discord, GitHub Sponsors, 2.7k stars, and a handful of recurring community contributors; solo primary maintainer.

ecosystem-crates.comment: Most ecosystem surface is first-party feature-gated crates (freya-devtools, freya-testing, freya-router, material-design, plotters integration, i18n, webview, terminal). Few third-party extensions exist yet.
